back_translation: Therefore, beloved ones, since we have these promises, let us cleanse ourselves to be pure from everything that defiles flesh and spirit, by causing holiness to be completed in the fear of God.
Open your hearts to receive us. We have not done wrong against anyone, we have not caused anyone to deteriorate, we have not taken advantage of anyone.
I do not say this to condemn you, for I have said before that you are in our hearts, so that we will die and live together with you.
I have great courage to speak with you, I take great pride in you, I have received encouragement fully, my joy overflows greatly amid all our affliction.
For when we arrived in the province of Macedonia, our flesh had no rest at all, but we were pressed in every direction — outside there were conflicts, inside there was fear.
But God who comforts the downcast comforted us by the arrival of Titus.

And not only by his arrival, but also by the encouragement that he received from you, while he reported to us about your great longing, your lamenting, and your zeal toward me, so that I rejoiced even more.
For even though I caused you sorrow by that letter, I do not regret it. Even though I once regretted it — because I see that the letter caused you sorrow only briefly —

Now I rejoice, not because you received sorrow, but because the sorrow led you to repentance, for you received sorrow according to God's will, so that you would not be harmed in any matter because of us.
For the sorrow that is according to God's will produces repentance leading to salvation, which need not be regretted afterward; but the sorrow of this world produces death.

For behold, this very fact that you received sorrow according to God's will has produced in you how much: earnestness, defense-statement, indignation, fear, great longing, zeal, and the bringing-about-of-justice. In every matter, you have proven yourselves to be innocent in this affair.

Therefore, even though I wrote to you, it was not on account of the one who did wrong, nor on account of the one who was wronged, but on account of the fact that your earnestness toward us would become clear before yourselves in the presence of God.
Because of this, we received encouragement. And beyond our own encouragement, we rejoiced even more because of Titus's joy, because his spirit had received refreshment from all of you.

For if I had taken pride in any matter concerning you before him, I did not have to be ashamed; on the contrary, just as we spoke everything to you with truth, our pride before Titus also became truth in the same way.
And his love toward you increased even more, when he remembered the obedience of all of you, that you welcomed him with reverent fear and trembling.
I rejoice that in every matter I am confident in you.
